2606.09323.title: TRL-Bench: Standardizing Cross-Paradigm Representation-Level Evaluation of Tabular Encoders
2606.09323.paper_summary: Tabular encoders are usually evaluated inside task-specific end-to-end pipelines, so models from different training paradigms are difficult to compare directly even when they operate on similar tabular signals. We introduce TRL-Bench, a multi-granular tabular representation learning (TRL) benchmark that standardizes cross-paradigm representation-level evaluation: each encoder exports row-, column-, or table embeddings through its supported wrapper, and shared lightweight heads probe them across three suites: TRL-CTbench (column/table), TRL-Rbench (row), and TRL-DLTE (compositional Data-Lake Table Enrichment spanning all three granularities). To support this standardized setting, we release curated benchmark assets and task reformulations, including 50 OpenML tables with 123 verified targets, 16 row-pair linkage rewrites, and a 47,772-table DLTE lake derived from 1,379 parent tables. Across 20 models and 16 tasks, TRL-Bench shows that once downstream conditions are standardized, encoder quality is capability-specific rather than captured by a single leaderboard. In TRL-CTbench, generic text encoders often lead on tasks with strong surface-text signal, while tabular specialists win where their pretraining objective aligns with the task. In TRL-Rbench, within-table prediction and cross-table linkage favor different training regimes, with atomic linkage performance correlating strongly with the row-matching stage of DLTE pipelines. In TRL-DLTE, the strongest pipelines combine capability-matched specialists rather than reuse a single encoder, and top end-to-end quality depends on non-additive compositional fit rather than per-stage marginal rank alone. TRL-Bench provides a common protocol for measuring reusable signal in exported tabular representations under shared downstream conditions. Code and data: https://github.com/LOGO-CUHKSZ/TRL-Bench
2606.09323.paper_summary_zh: 表格編碼器通常在特定任務的端到端流程中進行評估，因此即使在類似的表格信號上運作，來自不同訓練範式的模型也難以直接比較。我們介紹了TRL-Bench，一個多粒度的表格表示學習（TRL）基準，標準化跨範式的表示層級評估：每個編碼器通過其支持的包裝器導出行、列或表嵌入，並在三個套件中使用共享的輕量級頭進行探測：TRL-CTbench（列/表）、TRL-Rbench（行）和TRL-DLTE（跨越所有三種粒度的組合數據湖表增強）。為了支持這一標準化設置，我們發布了策劃的基準資產和任務重構，包括50個OpenML表格，擁有123個經過驗證的目標，16個行對鏈接重寫，以及從1,379個父表派生的47,772個表的DLTE湖。在20個模型和16個任務中，TRL-Bench顯示一旦下游條件標準化，編碼器的質量是特定於能力的，而不是由單一排行榜捕捉。在TRL-CTbench中，通用文本編碼器通常在具有強表面文本信號的任務中領先，而表格專家則在其預訓練目標與任務對齊的地方獲勝。在TRL-Rbench中，表內預測和跨表鏈接偏好不同的訓練範式，原子鏈接性能與DLTE流程的行匹配階段強烈相關。在TRL-DLTE中，最強的流程結合了能力匹配的專家，而不是重用單一編碼器，頂級的端到端質量取決於非加性組合擬合，而不僅僅是每個階段的邊際排名。TRL-Bench提供了一個共同的協議，用於在共享下游條件下測量導出表格表示中的可重用信號。代碼和數據：https://github.com/LOGO-CUHKSZ/TRL-Bench
2606.09323.author: Wei Pang et.al.
2606.09323.authors: Wei Pang, Xiangru Jian, Hehan Li, Zhixuan Yu, Alex Xue, Jinyang Li, Zhengyuan Dong, Xinjian Zhao, Hao Xu, Chao Zhang, Reynold Cheng, M. Tamer Özsu, Tianshu Yu
